gaps.description: seedStarterPlants accepts caller-supplied householdId and resolves targetHouseholdId = householdId ?? session.user.activeHouseholdId without calling requireHouseholdAccess. Any authenticated non-demo user can inject plants into any household by ID. Breaks Phase 02 goal #4 (All Server Actions enforce live household membership check).
gaps.fix: Add 'await requireHouseholdAccess(targetHouseholdId)' after the null-check on line 162. Add import { requireHouseholdAccess } from '@/features/household/guards' at top of file.

next_action: Close GAP-02-01 (seedStarterPlants auth bypass). Fastest path: /gsd-code-review-fix 02 --ws household. Canonical path: /gsd-plan-phase 2 --gaps --ws household → /gsd-execute-phase 2 --gaps-only --ws household → verifier re-runs. Either path ends with Phase 02 completion being marked in ROADMAP via `node gsd-tools.cjs phase complete 02 --ws household`.
context_notes: 10 plans executed across 7 waves using isolation=worktree in Claude Code. Key observations: (1) Claude Code's worktree isolation sometimes commits directly onto feat/household rather than the worktree branch — the manual `git merge worktree-agent-*` step was sometimes a no-op. (2) Wave 2 and Wave 3 exposed that parallel executors with overlapping test-file changes can leave cross-plan integration issues — the post-merge test gate caught a 16-test regression from 02-05b skipping the guards mock pattern in watering/notes tests. (3) 02-05b did cascade work beyond its planned scope (legacy redirect stubs + demo bootstrap) to make npm run build green. This was documented and accepted retroactively in 02-03b-SUMMARY.md. (4) The CR-01 critical finding (seedStarterPlants auth bypass) was not caught by Wave 7 (02-06 ForbiddenError tests) because 02-06 only covered the 13 mutating actions that DO call requireHouseholdAccess — seedStarterPlants was never listed because it was (incorrectly) treated as a demo-only internal helper. This is the structural gap the verifier flagged.
